text: Artificial intelligence is increasingly common in modern workplaces, bringing both opportunities and challenges. Many routine tasks are now automated, from data entry to customer service chatbots, which saves time and reduces human error. Productivity tools powered by AI help employees manage schedules, analyze large amounts of data, and generate reports quickly. However, rather than replacing workers entirely, most experts see a future of human-AI collaboration, where AI handles repetitive work while people focus on creative and strategic decisions. This does mean some jobs will change significantly, requiring workers to learn new skills or adapt their roles. As AI becomes more widespread, the ability to work alongside intelligent systems may become as important as traditional expertise.
